WH6N COOP OBSERVER REPORTED ALL PONDS AND DRAWS FULL OF WATER IN THE AREA BETWEEN WILD HORSE AND KIT CARSON THIS MORNING. ALSO HAD SEVERAL SECOND HAND REPORTS OF FLASH FLOODING IN AREA WITH AGRICULTURAL DAMS OVERTOPPING, WATER OVER HWY 287/40 LAST NIGHT, WATER FLOWING INTO A HOUSE BEING DIVERTED BY COUNTY CREW, AND HAIL SO BAD IT GOT SEVERAL SECTIONS OF GRASSLAND INCLUDING THE SAGEBRUSH WITH HAIL STILL ON THE GROUND THIS MORNING. DELAYED REPORT, TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
CORN CROP SEVERELY STRIPPED BY WIND DRIVEN SMALL HAIL THAT RANGED IN SIZE FROM PEAS TO DIMES. DELAYED REPORT TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
CAR IN DITCH FILLED WITH WATER ON LINCOLN ST. DRIVER UNAWARE IT WAS A DITCH AND NOT KNOWING HOW DEEP WATER WAS.

12 PINE TREES DOWN, ALL WERE 70 TO 80 FEET TALL. ONE TREE WAS BROKEN IN THREE PLACES. ONE TREES WAS PULLED OUT OF THE GROUND WITH THE ROOTS STILL ATTACHED. ALL WIRES WERE PULLED OFF INDIVIDUALS HOUSE DUE TO A TREE FALLING. 14 POWERLINES SHEARED OFF JUST NORTH OF THE RESIDENCE. ALL DAMAGE WAS IN ONE DIRECTION, TO THE SOUTHEAST. THERE WAS ALSO SOME SIGNIFICANT HAIL DAMAGE BUT A SIZE WAS NOT PROVIDED AS THE RESIDENT WAS NOT HOME AT THE TIME OF THE STORM. ONE CORN FIELD COMPLETELY DESTROYED NEARBY.
